message:
Scope extension filesystem access by extension ID and harden path traversal controls

Major security improvement: extension IDs now scope filesystem access to prevent
cross-extension data leakage and monorepo escape attacks.

Extension ID-Scoped Filesystem Access:
- New extension_roots_by_id HashMap stores per-extension-ID canonical roots
- current_extension_id() reads __pi_current_extension_id from JS globals
- extension_roots_for_fs_access() returns only the allowed roots for the
  currently active extension
- path_is_in_allowed_extension_root() performs bounds-checking against
  canonicalized allowed roots
- add_extension_root_with_id() canonicalizes paths, prevents duplicates,
  and stores roots by extension ID with legacy fallback

Monorepo Escape Detection:
- New detect_monorepo_escape() check prevents ../../ traversal across
  extension root boundaries
- Returns error with escaped path details when traversal detected

Path Traversal Test Fix:
- Replace eval_file() (runs as script, bypasses resolver) with dynamic
  import() (runs as module, applies resolver validation)
- Improved assertions verify both "Module path escapes extension root"
  and specific file name in error messages

State management updates: clears ID-scoped root maps on reset/transient clear.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
